data.title: The Trendlines Makes Investments in Portfolio Companies
data.description:
The Trendlines' board approved investments in its portfolio companies by the related companies of Librae Holdings, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Thursday.

Librae is a controlling shareholder of the company. Along with the company, Librae will invest in ProArc Medical and Sol Chip for a total of approximately $1.9 million.

data.title: Montenegro's Q1 games of chance revenue up 37% y/y
data.description:
PODGORICA (Montenegro), April 11 (SeeNews) - Montenegro's revenues from games of chance rose by an annual 37% to 9.0 million euro ($10 million) in the first quarter of 2025 thanks to improved market supervision mechanisms and the successful implementation of a new information system, the government said.

Revenue from online gambling jumped 70% in annual terms to 3.8 million euro in January-March, the administration for games of chance said in a statement earlier this week.

Revenue from betting totalled 2.5 million euro in the period under review, up by an annual 6.1%, while revenue from the operation of gambling machine clubs increased by 21% to 1.7 million euro.

Revenue from casino games rose 81% on the year to 1.0 million euro in the first three months of 2025.

In 2024, Montenegro's total games of chance revenue jumped 51% to 33.8 million euro on the back of a new variable component added by the government to the concession fee for online games of chance as of January 2024.

($ = 0.902 euro)

data.title: Jade Gas Holdings Progresses Drilling at Mongolia Gas Field, Shares Jump 21%
data.description:
Jade Gas Holdings progressed its gas production drilling program activities at the Red Lake gas field in Mongolia, according to a Friday Australian bourse filing.

It drilled a second lateral coal seam well to a total depth of 1,507 meters of measured depth, with a lateral section of around 902 meters. 802 meters of net coal was intersected in a target seam.

Both wells are now being equipped for production and are expected to be online and producing within the next three to four weeks, per the filing.

Its shares jumped above 21% in recent trading on Friday.

data.title: Google lays off hundreds of employees in Android, Pixel Group, The Information reports
data.description: Alphabet's Google on Thursday laid off hundreds of employees in its platforms and devices unit, which works on Android software, Pixel phones and the Chrome browser, The Information reported on Friday, citing a person with direct knowledge of the situation.

data.title: Bloomage BioTechnology 2024 Profit Falls 71%; Shares Down 3%
data.description:
Bloomage BioTechnology posted 2024 net profit attributable to shareholders of 174.3 million yuan, down 71% from 592.6 million yuan the previous year.

Earnings per share fell to 0.36 yuan from 1.23 yuan, according to a filing with the Shanghai bourse.

The biomedical products maker's revenue slipped 12% year over year to 5.37 billion yuan from 6.08 billion yuan.

Bloomage BioTechnology declared a dividend of 0.11 yuan per share.

Shares of the company were down 3% in recent trade.

data.title: Crypto gaming has mixed Q1 as deals jump, investment totals dip: DappRadar
data.description:
Blockchain gaming for the first quarter of 2025 has been a “mixed bag,” seeing a greater number of deals while the amount invested significantly dipped, says blockchain analytics platform DappRadar.

Web3 gaming projects raised $91 million in Q1 2025, marking a 71% decrease from the fourth quarter of 2024 and a 68% drop compared to the same quarter a year ago, DappRadar said in its April 10 State of Blockchain Gaming report.

DappRadar analyst Sara Gherghelas wrote the figures showed “the growing pressure on early-stage startups and hint that 2025 may prove more challenging than previous years — unless broader market conditions improve.”

Another factor for the drop in investments in blockchain games is investors are increasingly shifting toward real-world assets and artificial intelligence, according to Gherghelas.

Over the same time, the number of blockchain gaming-related deals that closed increased by 35% quarter-over-quarter.

Gherghelas said the jump in deals shows that “while investors are writing smaller checks, they're still actively engaging with a broader range of projects — indicating continued interest, albeit with more cautious allocation."

Web3 gaming investors go big in infrastructure

The lion’s share of funding for Web3 gaming in the first quarter went to infrastructure-focused projects, with most focused on scalable gaming infrastructure, according to the report.

Gherghelas said the focus on infrastructure funding signaled that “investor confidence in the long-term potential of Web3 gaming remains intact,” with a few stand-out projects in the quarter, such as those from MARBLEX and The Game Company.

MARBLEX, the blockchain gaming division of South Korean game developer Netmarble, has plans for a Semi-Publishing Model to support a wider variety of Web3 games, backed by a joint fund exceeding $20 million with Immutable. 

Meanwhile, Dubai-based startup The Game Company, a firm focused on blockchain-based cloud gaming, received $10 million in funding on Feb. 6 to help develop a platform that allows users to play any game on any device.

Gherghelas said that as the Web gaming industry matures, there is “a clear push toward quality, innovation, and interoperability — whether through upgraded gameplay, new identity layers, or AI-enhanced mechanics.”

data.title: Stocks skid as U.S.-China trade war keeps investors on edge, currencies firm
data.description:
Key points:
  • Sell-off resumes in Asia EM equities
  • Currencies firm against a soft dollar
  • Singapore c.bank to ease next week - Reuters poll
  • Xi Jinping to visit Southeast Asia next week

Stocks in Asian emerging markets slipped on Friday as investor anxiety over tariff uncertainty and the broader fallout from an escalating U.S.-China trade war prompted a pullback from riskier assets.

But there was relief for regional currencies as the U.S. dollar fell below 100 against a basket of major units for the first time since July 2023.

The Singapore dollar firmed to its highest level in more than five months, while Malaysia's ringgit appreciated by 0.7%. The Philippine peso hit a one-week high, while Indonesia's rupiah was largely flat.

An MSCI index of emerging market currencies (.MIEM00000CUS) hit a one-week high.

"A broadly weaker dollar amid a trade slowdown makes the monetary policy picture more clear for EM central banks," said Alex Wolf, head of Asia Investment Strategy at J.P. Morgan Private Bank.

"A weaker USD driven by financial outflows as global investors soured on the U.S. exceptionalism has boosted EM currencies (with a few exceptions), providing central banks more leeway in setting policy."

On Thursday, the Philippine central bank resumed its easing cycle, cutting its key interest rate by a quarter-point to help the economy. Next week, Singapore's central bank is widely expected to reduce the slope of its policy band by at least 50 basis points.

Traders resumed the sell-off in Asian emerging equities a day after a rally prompted by U.S. President Donald Trump's abrupt announcement of a 90-day pause in hefty import tariffs.

But Trump's erratic tariff policies and their potential economic fallout has eroded appetite for risk, with the intensifying trade war with China , Southeast Asia's largest trade partner, keeping traders on edge.

Singapore's benchmark gauge dropped as much as 3%, a day after the index advanced more than 5%. Stocks in Malaysia and the Philippines fell around 2% each, while Indonesian shares posted marginal losses.

The MSCI gauge of Asian emerging market equities (.MIMS00000PUS) edged lower, while the FTSE ASEAN 40 index (.FTASEAN40) shed 1%, pulling back from a more than 5% surge in the previous session.

Most gauges were set to end the week deep in the red.

After days of market carnage that scorched everything from the dollar to U.S. Treasuries, usually the global safe-haven, investors were scooping up high-quality assets.

"We are finding best-in-class stocks across sectors and markets that usually trade at a premium, (and are) starting to look attractive relative to their own history," said Rushil Khanna, head of equity investments for Asia at Ostrum, an affiliate of Natixis Investment Managers.

"While defensive, dividend and domestically orientated stocks seem to be a consensus buy, high quality growth stocks are also looking attractive and offer a margin of safety for the patient investor."

HIGHLIGHTS:

** Yields on most Southeast Asian 10-year bonds edge higher

** China's Xi to visit Southeast Asia as trade conflict with U.S. deepens

** Malaysia to await tariffs clarity before revising outlook, c.bank says

** Facing Trump tariffs, Vietnam eyes crackdown on some China trade

data.title: Viromed Medical Lands Exclusive Distribution Deal for Veterinary Medical Device
data.description:
Viromed Medical signed an exclusive distribution deal with Lang Life Sciences for the former's ViroCAP system, a medical device to treat skin diseases in animals.

Under the deal, Lang Life Sciences will acquire 1,000 ViroCAP systems over the first 12 months and market them in Germany, Austria, and Switzerland under its brand and veterinary platform Amamus Vet, the German medical product developer said Thursday.

Deliveries are planned to begin at the end of August.

data.title: Guobo Electronics 2024 Profit Down 20%; Shares Rise 6%
data.description:
Guobo Electronics posted 2024 net profit attributable to shareholders of 484.6 million yuan, down 20% from 606.2 million yuan the previous year.

Earnings per share fell to 0.81 yuan from 1.02 yuan, according to a filing with the Shanghai bourse.

The radio frequency components maker's revenue declined 27% year over year to 2.59 billion yuan from 3.57 billion yuan.

Guobo Electronics declared a dividend of 0.40 yuan per share.

Shares of the company were up 6% in recent trade.

data.title: Turkey - Factors To Watch on April 11
data.description:
Here are news, reports and events that may affect Turkish financial markets on Friday.

The lira traded at 38.0620 against the U.S. dollar at 0405 GMT, weakening from its close of 37.94 on Thursday.

The main BIST 100 share index closed 0.68% higher at 9,338.58 points on Thursday.

GLOBAL MARKETS

Global stocks slumped and the dollar sank further on Friday, while a manic bond selloff took hold in a brutal end to the week of tit-for-tat worldwide tariffs that have fed fears of a deep recession and shaken investor confidence in U.S. assets.

DIPLOMACY FORUM

Turkey hosts the Antalya Diplomacy Forum in the Mediterranean city of Antalya on April 11-13, with various foreign leaders attending including Syria's President Ahmed al-Sharaa and Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov. Turkish President Tayyip Erdogan is expected to deliver a speech.

Turkish Vice President Cevdet Yilmaz, Foreign Minister Hakan Fidan, Finance Minister Mehmet Simsek will attend the opening of Antalya Diplomacy Forum.

GAZA CONFLICT

Turkish Foreign Minister Hakan Fidan hosts Palestinian, Saudi, Qatari and other counterparts to discuss a "two-state solution and lasting peace" related the Israeli attacks on Gaza, with Fidan later holding a press conference.

ENERGY MINISTER

Turkey's Energy Minister Alparslan Bayraktar will attend to IICEC Energy and Climate Forum.

IMAMOGLU HEARING

Ekrem Imamoglu, the mayor of Istanbul and main rival of President Tayyip Erdogan, faces his first hearing over accusations leveled against him before he was jailed last month over separate charges that caused a political crisis and market selloff.

In the trial beginning on Friday, when his supporters are expected to protest outside his prison, Imamoglu is accused of insulting and threatening a public official in remarks criticising the Istanbul prosecutor in recent months.

CENTRAL BANK SURVEY (TRINTM=ECI)

The central bank will release its monthly survey of market participants' expectations for the economy (0700 GMT).

data.title: Iltani Resources Receives Over AU$230,000 Grant for Airborne Geophysical Survey at Herberton Project in Queensland
data.description:
Iltani Resources received a AU$230,375 grant from the Queensland government to fund an airborne geophysical survey at its Herberton project, according to a Friday filing with the Australian bourse.

The grant is through Round 9 of the Collaborative Exploration Initiative under the Queensland Department of Natural Resources and Mines, Manufacturing and Regional and Rural Development's Industry Development Plan, the filing stated.

The survey is expected to be completed in May or June. Results will be used to guide further exploration at Orient and regional exploration across the Herberton project, the filing added.

Iltani Resources' shares were up 2% in recent trade.

data.title: Back to white-knuckle ride in the markets
data.description:
A look at the day ahead in European and global markets from Ankur Banerjee

Fears of a sharp downturn in the global economy have sent markets convulsing once more, with action focused on currencies and bonds as the escalating U.S.-China trade war has investors throwing out the usual playbook and fleeing dollar-based assets.

Stock futures in Europe were pointing to a subdued open but the Swiss franc reached a 10-year high and the yen was its strongest in six months. Gold prices resumed their march to successive record highs and the euro rose to levels not seen since February 2022.

King dollar no more, perhaps.

After an all-too-brief relief rally that followed U.S. President Donald Trump's temporary retreat from some of his tariff threats, Asian stock markets from Japan to Australia were showing a sea of red. Stock markets in China, the remaining target of Trump's wrath, were nevertheless relatively steady.

Relentless selling resumed in the dollar and bonds, with the 10-year note yield rising to 4.444% and on course for its biggest weekly increase since 2001, LSEG data showed. Thirty-year bond yields (US30YT=RR) are set for their biggest weekly jump since at least 1982.

This week's volatile reaction in the bond market has called into question Treasury bonds' status as the world's safest asset. So, what are the safe assets now?

Apart from the surge in currencies such as the yen and Swiss franc, gold prices have lurched to another record above $3,200 per ounce. The yellow metal's rise shows no signs of stopping as safe haven flows keep on coming.

Key developments that could influence markets on Friday:

Economic events: March inflation data for Germany, Feb GDP estimate for UK, tariff updates

Trying to keep up with the latest tariff news?

Our new daily news digest offers a rundown of the top market-moving headlines impacting global trade. Sign up for Tariff Watch here.

data.title: India stocks, rupee, swaps, call at 10:00 a.m. IST
data.description:
STOCKS:

The benchmark BSE Sensex was up 2% to 75,284, while the broader NSE Nifty 50 rose 2% to 22864, as a U.S. tariff reprieve lifted sentiment, even as global trade uncertainty continues to linger.

RUPEE:

The Indian rupee rose 0.6% versus the U.S. dollar to 86.1250, tracking gains in Asian peers as the dollar slumped on the back of concerns about a trade-war spurred slowdown in the world's largest economy.

GOVERNMENT BONDS:

The benchmark 10-year bond (IN067934G=CC) was quoted at 102.44 rupees, with the yield slightly lower at 6.4417% with as traders awaiting a debt sale later in the day.

OVERNIGHT INDEX SWAPS:

The one-year overnight index swap rate (INR1YMIBROIS=CC) was down 4 basis points at 5.78%, while the five-year swap rate (INR5YMIBROIS=CC) declined nearly 4 bps to 5.7125%.

data.title: Indian metal stocks rally on weaker dollar
data.description:
** India metal stocks up ~4%, top gainer among the 13 major indices

** Dollar index lower at 100, fell 2% on Thursday as trade war worries resurfaced after a brief rally due to 90-day pause in U.S. reciprocal tariffs

** Weaker dollar makes metals like steel and copper cheaper for foreign buyers, boosting demand

** All 15 index constituents in green, with Tata Steel , JSW Steel and Hindalco the top gainers, up between 4%-5%

** Including session's gains, NIFTYMET has declined about 10% in April due to fall in base metal prices on fears that tariffs will hit global growth

data.title: Netwealth Looking to Double Down on ReinvestmentMarket Talk
data.description: Australia's Netwealth is giving clear indication that it intends to double down on its attempt to capture the opportunity on offer, Wilsons analysts tell clients in a note. They observe that management's reiteration that they intend to focus on reinvestment in fiscal 2026 demonstrates confidence in the medium-term outlook. This is supported by improving cadence in net flows despite the current risk-off sentiment in markets, they say. Wilsons raises its target price for the stock by 10% to A$27.66 and keeps an overweight recommendation on the stock. Shares are down 5.35% at A$24.95. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

data.title: Anand Rathi Wealth Clocks Gains in Fiscal Q4 Consolidated Profit
data.description:
Anand Rathi Wealth recorded a rise in its consolidated attributable net profit to 734.5 million Indian rupees in the fiscal fourth quarter ended March 31, against 566.0 million rupees a year ago.

Earnings per share climbed to 8.87 rupees compared with the year-ago figure of 6.80 rupees, according to a Thursday filing to the Indian stock exchanges by the stock broking company.

Revenue from operations in fiscal Q4 increased to 221.96 billion rupees from 184.28 billion rupees a year earlier.

The company's board declared a final dividend of 7 rupees per equity share of face value of 5 rupees each for the financial year 2024-2025.

data.title: OCBC Uses Treasury Shares for Employees' Share Schemes
data.description:
Oversea-Chinese Banking Corp. or OCBC used another 12,328 treasury shares worth around SG$182,094 for its employees' share schemes on Thursday, a same-day Singapore bourse filing said.

Following the transaction, the number of treasury shares stood at 15,295,447 shares, the lender said.

data.title: Market Chatter: South Korea to Expand Trade Task Force After US Tariff Pause
data.description:
South Korea will broaden its response task force into a full interagency team to address changing US tariff policies, Yonhap News Agency reported Thursday.

The move follows Washington's 90-day suspension of newly imposed reciprocal tariffs, just hours after they took effect, according to the report.

The expanded task force will include eight sector-specific working groups covering areas such as macroeconomy, finance, industry, and SMEs, the report said.

The group aims to strengthen analysis and coordination across agencies and explore longer-term trade strategies, according to the report.

Disclaimer

Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.

data.title: Casa Holdings Flags Net Loss for Six Months Ended March 31
data.description:
Casa Holdings is expected to book a net loss for the six months ended March 31, according to a Thursday filing with the Singapore Exchange.

The company has attributed this to foreign exchange losses and the appreciation of the Malaysia ringgit against the Singapore dollar.

The company will release its financial results on or before May 15.

data.title: Alpina Holdings Receives Demand Letter Over Deferred Payment
data.description:
Alpina Holdings received a letter of demand from warrantors regarding a deferred payment of around SG$1.8 million, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Thursday.

The warrantors disagree with the company's view that it is not obliged to make the deferred payment, prompting them to seek arbitration proceedings.

This development comes after the company agreed to acquire the Wan Dormitory for SG$24.5 million and completed the purchase in March.

The company had withheld a deferred payment of SG$2 million from the consideration at completion. The payment was slated to be released within ten business days after fulfilling all deal conditions and completing the SLA tender assignment.

data.title: China's SME Performance Rises in Q1
data.description:
The performance of Chinese small and medium-sized enterprises improved in the first quarter of the year, Xinhua reported Thursday, citing data from the China Association of Small and Medium Enterprises.

The Small and Medium Enterprises Development Index rose to 89.5 from 89 in the previous quarter as consumption during the holidays bolstered, the report said.

The sub-indices for industry, wholesale and retail, transport, social services, and information transmission software all expanded quarter on quarter. Only the construction sub-index remained flat, the report said.

Meanwhile, real estate, accommodation, and catering sub-indices kept on declining, the report said.

data.title: African Markets - Factors to watch on April 11
data.description:
The following company announcements, scheduled economic indicators, debt and currency market moves and political events may affect African markets on Friday.

- - - - -

GLOBAL MARKETS

Global stocks slumped and the dollar sank further on Friday, while a manic bond selloff took hold in a brutal end to the week of tit-for-tat worldwide tariffs that have fed fears of a deep recession and shaken investor confidence in U.S. assets.

WORLD OIL PRICES

Oil prices fell on Friday and were set to drop for a second week on concerns prolonged trade war between the United States and China, the world's largest economies, will crush crude consumption as their dispute curtails economic growth.

EMERGING MARKETS

For the top emerging markets news, double click on

AFRICA STOCKS

AFRICA MARKETS

The Nigerian and Ghanaian currencies are expected to be broadly stable in the next week to Thursday thanks to central bank support, while the Ugandan and Zambian currencies could fall, traders said.

SOUTH AFRICA MARKETS

South Africa's rand stumbled on Thursday on global market jitters while stocks jumped, as investors digested U.S. President Donald Trump's announcement of an immediate 90-day tariff pause for many countries.

KENYA MARKETS

Kenya's shilling gained ground against the dollar on Thursday, after U.S. President Donald Trump's unexpected decision to temporarily suspend most of his recently imposed tariffs, traders said.

SUDAN GENOCIDE

Sudan told the International Court of Justice on Thursday that the United Arab Emirates was violating the Genocide Convention by supporting paramilitary forces in Darfur, but the UAE argued the case should be thrown out as the court lacked jurisdiction.

US AID

President Donald Trump's administration wants the U.N. World Food Programme to modify operations backed by the U.S. in six countries, a State Department spokesperson said on Wednesday, after Washington terminated and then reinstated the assistance.

TANZANIA POLITICS

Tanzanian opposition party leader Tundu Lissu was on Thursday charged with treason, a capital offence, for comments he made last week that prosecutors said called on the public to launch a rebellion and disrupt an election due this year.

data.title: U.K. Shopper Traffic Drops in March on Late Easter, Report Says
data.description:
By Michael Susin

U.K. retail footfall fell in March, dragged by a late Easter and ongoing subdued consumer confidence, according to a report published Friday.

The number of visits to stores--comprising main-street shops, retail parks and shopping centers--for the five weeks ended April 5 decreased by 5.4% from the same period a year earlier, according to the report by the British Retail Consortium and Sensormatic Solutions IQ. This compares with the 0.2% increase in February.

The figure, however, represents an on-year distortion given that Easter this year is in April, while in 2024 it was in March. This results in an artificially higher footfall in April, but a lower figure in March.

Footfall at retail parks saw the smallest drop, down 1.2% in March, compared with a 2.0% rise in February. The segment is managing to hold up better than other segments thanks to the offer of hospitality and leisure alongside retail, BRC Chief Executive Helen Dickinson said.

Shopping-center visits decreased by 5.8%, compared with a 0.1% rise the prior month, and main-street stores footfall fell 4.0%, a slight increase of 0.1% from the month prior.

Footfall also decreased across all nations, with Northern Ireland seeing the largest drop, at 9.0%, while England had the smallest decrease of 4.9%.

Looking ahead, global uncertainties regarding tariffs and a potential economic slowdown could further erode consumer sentiment in the coming months, Dickinson added.

Write to Michael Susin at michael.susin@wsj.com
